text:
Once upon a time in sleepy Owl Creek town, there lived a wise owl named Oliver. He knew everything. Well, most things.

Every morning, Oliver would perch atop his favourite oak. He loved watching the world wake up. Birds squawk. Leaves rustle. Breakfast thieves stealing cheese. Today was harvest time.

He saw farmers. They had big machines. Trees. Harvest!

Olivers hatched a plan. No more fruits. Just grains.

He spread seeds throughout Whispering Woodlands. Quiet owls took them. He listened. Owls came.

But one.

A little.

Too little.

Owl swallowed seeds. Maybe.

He flew.

No.

Too dizzy.

Sleepy.

Oliver dropped seeds.

Flocks. Owlets.

He'd save.

Then he'd.

Fly.

Late owl.

No. Night owl.

He heard.

A tale.

Like him.

A dreamer. Poor. Brave.

I’ll protect.

His kind.

No.

You'll steal.
